Ntt docomo inc.周四说，与该公司电子货币服务相关的银行账户约人民币1800万元被盗，这促使警方开始对一起涉嫌诈骗的案件展开调查。
NTT Docomo副总裁丸山诚司(Seiji Maruyama)在东京的新闻发布会上表示，截至周四，已确认66起与移动运营商电子货币服务相关的银行账户不当提款案件。
丸山在新闻发布会上说，我们向不正当提款的受害者表示歉意。其他公司高管也参加了新闻发布会。
NTT Docomo已停止允许客户在其电子货币服务与35家合作银行的账户之间创建新的链接。该公司表示，将试图通过与银行的谈判，赔偿受害者被盗的全部金额。
去年5月，与NTT Docomo的电子货币服务相关的Resona银行账户也出现过类似的不当提款案例。DoCoMo承认，它未能加强用户身份检查，以防止再次发生。
它补充说，在35家合作银行中，7家地区性银行已经停止了与电子货币服务的所有交易。
这项服务的用户可以使用他们的智能手机在商店付款和转账，如果他们将这项服务与他们的银行账户联系起来，就可以很容易地获得电子货币。
任何人都可以通过在NTT Docomo的会员系统中注册电子邮件地址来使用这项服务，这意味着身份检查不像对手机用户那样严格。
在最近的案件中，相信有第三者取得受害人的银行户口号码和密码，并利用它们在电子货币服务上登记以转账。
NTT Docomo表示，为了防止再次发生这种情况，它将引入额外的识别步骤，比如要求新用户注册他们的手机号码。
该公司还表示，将引入一次性密码，通过短信服务发送到用户的手机，并使用带照片的身份证进行更严格的身份识别。
一次最多可以从银行账户向电子货币服务汇款10万元。据NTT Docomo称，上限设定为每月30万元，而在Resona案件发生后，上限从100万元下调。

  About ¥18 million has been stolen from bank accounts linked to NTT Docomo Inc.’s e-money service, the company said Thursday, prompting police to begin an investigation into a suspected scam.
 As of Thursday, 66 cases of improper withdrawals from bank accounts linked to the mobile carrier’s e-money service had been confirmed, NTT Docomo Vice President Seiji Maruyama told a news conference in Tokyo.
  “We apologize to the victims” of the improper withdrawals, Maruyama said at the news conference, which was also attended by other company executives.
  NTT Docomo, which has stopped allowing customers to create new links between its e-money service and accounts at 35 partner banks, has said it will try to compensate victims for the full amounts stolen through negotiations with the banks.
   In May last year, there were similar cases of improper withdrawals from Resona Bank accounts linked to NTT Docomo’s e-money service. Docomo acknowledged it had failed to boost user identity checks to prevent a recurrence.
 Among the 35 partner banks, seven regional lenders have stopped all transactions with the e-money service, it added.
 Users of the service can use their smartphones for payments at shops and for money transfers, and can easily make e-money available if they link the service to their bank accounts.
 Anyone can use the service by registering an e-mail address with NTT Docomo’s membership system, meaning identity checks are not as strict as for mobile phone subscribers.
 In the recent cases, third parties are believed to have obtained the victims’ bank account numbers and passwords, and used them to register with the e-money service to transfer funds.
 To prevent a recurrence, NTT Docomo said it will introduce additional identification steps, such as requiring new users to register their mobile phone numbers.
 The company also said it will introduce one-time passwords to be sent to users’ mobile phones via a text messaging service, and a tighter identification process using photo identity cards.
 A maximum of ¥100,000 can be sent from a bank account to the e-money service at one time. The upper limit is set at ¥300,000 a month, which was lowered from ¥1 million after the cases at Resona, according to NTT Docomo.
title: 日本NTT Docomo承认小偷伪造了其电子货币服务
title_en: Japan's NTT Docomo Admits Thieves Breeched Its e-Money Service

三名直接了解此事的人士周五表示，北京方面反对中国所有者字节跳动(ByteDance)强行出售TikTok&39；在美国的业务，并更希望看到这款短视频应用在美国关闭。
自从美国总统特朗普上个月威胁如果TikTok&#39；不出售将禁止该服务以来，ByteDance一直在洽谈将TikTok&#美国业务出售给包括微软(Microsoft)和甲骨文(Oracle)在内的潜在买家。
三名直接了解此事的人士周五表示，北京方面反对中国所有者字节跳动(ByteDance)强行出售TikTok&39；在美国的业务，并更希望看到这款短视频应用在美国关闭。
自从美国总统特朗普上个月威胁如果TikTok&#39；不出售将禁止该服务以来，ByteDance一直在洽谈将TikTok&#美国业务出售给包括微软(Microsoft)和甲骨文(Oracle)在内的潜在买家。
然而，消息人士称，中国官员认为，面对华盛顿的压力，强行出售将使ByteDance和中国都显得软弱。鉴于局势的敏感性，消息人士要求匿名。
ByteDance在给路透的声明中称，中国政府从未向其建议关闭美国或任何其他市场的TikTok。
其中两名消息人士表示，如果有必要，中国愿意利用8月28日对技术出口清单的修改，推迟ByteDance达成的任何协议。
中国国务院新闻办公室及其外交部和商务部没有立即回应下班后发出的置评请求。
周五，当被问及特朗普和TikTok时，中国外交部发言人赵立建在例行新闻发布会上表示，美国正在滥用国家安全概念，并敦促美国停止压迫外国公司。
路透社(Reuters)报道称，TikTok的潜在买家正在讨论从ByteDance进行收购的四种方式。
其中，ByteDance仍可以在没有关键算法的情况下，在没有中国商务部批准的情况下推动TikTok美国资产的出售。
字节跳动及其创始人张一鸣陷入了世界两大强国之间的冲突。
特朗普上个月发布了两项行政命令，要求ByteDance出售TikTok&Tok；的美国资产，否则将在美国被禁止。在美国，这款应用在青少年中非常受欢迎。
美国官员批评了这款应用的安全性和隐私性，暗示用户数据可能会与北京方面共享。TikTok表示，它不会遵守任何与中国当局共享用户数据的要求。
北京方面表示，它坚决反对特朗普的行政命令，并在8月28日采取行动，让自己在这一过程中拥有发言权，修改了一份需要中国政府批准才能出口的技术清单。专家表示，TikTok的推荐算法将属于这一名单。
中国监管机构上周表示，这些规定不是针对特定公司的，但他们重申了自己执行这些规定的权利。
note_en:
Beijing opposes a forced sale of TikTok's U.S. operations by its Chinese owner ByteDance, and would prefer to see the short video app shut down in the United States, three people with direct knowledge of the matter said on Friday.
 ByteDance has been in talks to sell TikTok's U.S. business to potential buyers including Microsoft and Oracle since U.S. President Donald Trump threatened last month to ban the service if it was not sold.
 Beijing opposes a forced sale of TikTok's U.S. operations by its Chinese owner ByteDance, and would prefer to see the short video app shut down in the United States, three people with direct knowledge of the matter said on Friday.
 ByteDance has been in talks to sell TikTok's U.S. business to potential buyers including  Microsoft and  Oracle since U.S. President Donald Trump threatened last month to ban the service if it was not sold.
   However, Chinese officials believe a forced sale would make both ByteDance and China appear weak in the face of pressure from Washington, the sources said, speaking on condition of anonymity given the sensitivity of the situation.
 ByteDance said in a statement to Reuters that the Chinese government had never suggested to it that it should shut down TikTok in the United States or in any other markets.
 Two of the sources said China was willing to use revisions it made to a technology exports list on Aug. 28 to delay any deal reached by ByteDance, if it had to.
 China's State Council Information Office and its foreign and commerce ministries did not immediately respond to requests for comment sent after working hours.
  Asked on Friday about Trump and TikTok, Chinese foreign ministry spokesman Zhao Lijian said at a regular press briefing that the United States was abusing the concept of national security, and urged it to stop oppressing foreign companies.
  Reuters has reported that TikTok's prospective buyers were discussing four ways to structure an acquisition from ByteDance.
 Within these, ByteDance could still push ahead with a sale of TikTok's U.S. assets without approval from China's commerce ministry by selling them without key algorithms.
 ByteDance and its founder Zhang Yiming have been caught in a clash between the world's two preeminent powers.
 Trump last month issued two executive orders that require ByteDance to sell TikTok's U.S. assets or face being banned in the country, where the app is hugely popular among teenagers.
 U.S. officials have criticised the app's security and privacy, suggesting that user data might be shared with Beijing. TikTok has said it would not comply with any request to share user data with the Chinese authorities.
 Beijing has said it firmly opposes Trump's executive orders and on Aug. 28 moved to give itself a say in the process, revising a list of technologies that will need Chinese government approval before they are exported. Experts said TikTok's recommendation algorithm would fall under this list.
 Chinese regulators said last week the rules were not targeted at specific companies but they reaffirmed their right to enforce them.

note:
跳到导航跳到搜索Terrance Stanley Fox CC OD(1958年7月28日-1981年6月28日)是一位加拿大运动员、人道主义和癌症研究活动家。1980年，由于癌症截肢了一条腿，他开始了一次从东到西横跨加拿大的长跑，以筹集资金和提高对癌症研究的认识。尽管他的癌症的扩散最终迫使他在143天和5373公里(3339英里)后结束了他的探索，并最终夺去了他的生命，但他的努力给世界带来了持久的遗产。一年一度的特里·福克斯跑步活动于1981年首次举行，现已发展到60多个国家的数百万参与者，现在是世界上最大的癌症研究单日筹款活动；截至2018年1月，以他的名义筹集的资金已超过7.5亿加元。[1]。
福克斯是一名长跑运动员，也是他所在的科奎拉姆港高中(现在以他的名字命名)和西蒙·弗雷泽大学(Simon Fraser University)的篮球运动员。1977年，在他被诊断出患有骨肉瘤后，他的右腿被截肢，尽管他继续使用假腿跑步。他还在温哥华打轮椅篮球，赢得了三次全国锦标赛冠军。
1980年，他开始了希望马拉松，这是一项为癌症研究筹集资金的越野长跑。他希望从加拿大2400万人中每人筹集1美元。4月份，他从圣约翰、纽芬兰和拉布拉多出发，几乎没有大张旗鼓地开始，每天都相当于跑了一场全程马拉松。当福克斯到达安大略省时，他已经成为全国的明星；他多次与商人、运动员和政客一起公开露面，努力筹集资金。当癌症扩散到他的肺部时，他被迫结束了在桑德湾外的跑步。9个月后，当他去世时，他战胜疾病和完成马拉松的希望破灭了。
除了是有史以来最年轻的加拿大友谊勋章获得者外，福克斯还获得了1980年卢·马什奖(Lou Marsh Award)的全国最佳运动员称号，并在1980年和1981年两次被评为加拿大年度新闻人物。他被认为是民族英雄，全国各地都有许多建筑、雕像、道路和公园以他的名字命名。
特里·福克斯1958年7月28日出生在马尼托巴省的温尼伯，父母是罗兰和贝蒂·福克斯。罗兰是加拿大国家铁路的道岔。[2]狐狸有一个哥哥弗雷德，一个弟弟达雷尔和一个妹妹朱迪丝。[3]福克斯的外祖母是梅蒂斯，福克斯的弟弟达雷尔拥有正式的梅蒂斯身份。[4]。
他的家人于1966年搬到不列颠哥伦比亚省的萨里，然后于1968年在科奎拉姆港定居。[3]他的父母忠于家庭，他的母亲特别保护她的孩子；正是通过她，福克斯养成了他对自己承诺的任何任务的顽强奉献精神。[5]他的父亲回忆说，他非常好胜，并指出福克斯非常讨厌失败，以至于他会继续任何活动，直到他成功。[6]。
他是一名热情的运动员，小时候踢足球、橄榄球和棒球。[7]福克斯热爱篮球，虽然他当时只有5英尺高，而且是一名差劲的球员，但他试图在八年级时加入校队。[8][9][8][9][8][9]玛丽·希尔初中的福克斯体育老师兼篮球教练鲍勃·麦吉尔觉得自己更适合做一名长跑运动员，并鼓励他参加这项运动。福克斯对越野跑没有兴趣，但他之所以参加，是因为他尊重并想取悦他的教练。[10]他决心继续打篮球，即使他是队里最后一个替补队员。福克斯在他的八年级赛季只打了一分钟，但他把夏天都用来提高他的发挥。他在九年级成为一名正式球员，并在十年级赢得了一个首发位置。[11]在12年级时，他和他最好的朋友道格·阿尔沃德一起获得了高中年度最佳运动员奖。[3]。
虽然他最初不确定自己是否想上大学，但福克斯的母亲说服他进入西蒙·弗雷泽大学(Simon Fraser University)学习，在那里他学习了人体运动学，作为成为一名体育教师的垫脚石。[12]他参加了少年校队篮球队的选拔，由于他的决心，他在更多有才华的球员面前赢得了一席之地。[3]
1976年11月12日，当福克斯驾车前往高贵林港莫里尔街的家中时，他被附近的桥梁建设分散了注意力，并撞上了一辆皮卡车的后座。当他的车无法驾驶时，福克斯出现时只有一个右膝酸痛。去年12月，他再次感到疼痛，但他选择忽略它，直到篮球赛季结束。[13]到1977年3月，疼痛加剧，他终于去了医院，在那里他被诊断出患有骨肉瘤，这是一种通常始于膝盖附近的癌症。[3]福克斯认为他的车祸削弱了他的膝盖，使他很容易感染这种疾病，尽管他的医生认为这与疾病没有关系。[14]他被告知，他的腿必须截肢，他需要化疗，最近的医学进步意味着他有50%的生存机会。福克斯了解到，两年前，这个数字只有15%；存活率的提高让他印象深刻，癌症研究的价值。[15]。
在假腿的帮助下，福克斯在截肢三周后开始行走。[3]然后，他和父亲一起打高尔夫球。[16]医生们对福克斯的乐观态度印象深刻，称这有助于他迅速康复。[17]他忍受了16个月的化疗，并发现他在不列颠哥伦比亚省癌症控制机构的设施中度过的时间很艰难，因为他看着其他癌症患者遭受痛苦，死于这种疾病。[18]福克斯带着新的目标结束了他的治疗：他觉得自己的生存要归功于医学的进步，并希望以一种能帮助他人找到勇气的方式生活。[19]。
1977年夏天，在加拿大轮椅运动协会工作的里克·汉森邀请福克斯参加他的轮椅篮球队的选拔赛。[20]虽然当时他正在接受化疗，但福克斯的活力给汉森留下了深刻的印象。[3]在学习如何玩这项运动不到两个月后，福克斯被任命为埃德蒙顿全国锦标赛的一员。[21]他随队赢得了三次全国冠军，并在1980年被北美轮椅篮球协会评为全明星。[22]。
在接受癌症手术的前一天晚上，福克斯收到了一篇关于迪克·特拉姆(Dick Traum)的文章，他是第一位完成纽约市马拉松比赛的截肢者。[3]这篇文章激励了他；他开始了为期14个月的训练计划，告诉他的家人他计划自己参加马拉松比赛。[2]私下里，他设计了一个更广泛的计划。他的住院经历让福克斯对用于癌症研究的资金如此之少感到愤怒。他打算跑完加拿大全程，希望提高人们对癌症的认识，这个目标最初只透露给了他的朋友道格拉斯·阿尔沃德(Douglas Alward)。[23]。
狐狸以一种不同寻常的步态跑步，因为他被要求用他的好腿跳起来，因为他的假肢上的弹簧每走一步都需要额外的时间来复位。[24]他发现训练很痛苦，因为他不得不对他的健腿和残肢施加额外的压力，导致骨头瘀伤、水泡和剧烈疼痛。福克斯发现，每次跑步大约20分钟后，他就越过了疼痛阈值，跑步变得更容易了。[25]。
1979年9月2日，福克斯在乔治王子参加了17英里(27公里)的公路比赛。他以最后一名的成绩结束比赛，落后他最接近的竞争对手10分钟，但他的努力得到了其他参与者的泪水和掌声。[3]马拉松之后，他向家人透露了他的全部计划。[26]他的母亲阻止了他，激怒了福克斯，尽管她后来来支持这个项目。她回忆说，他说，我以为你会是最早信任我的人之一，而我不是。我是第一个让他失望的人。[27]福克斯最初希望筹集100万美元，然后是1000万美元，但后来寻求为加拿大2400万公民每人筹集1美元。[28][28]。
1979年10月15日，福克斯给加拿大癌症协会发了一封信，在信中他宣布了自己的目标，并呼吁提供资金。他说他要克服他的残疾，并承诺要完成他的跑步，即使他不得不爬过每一英里。在解释为什么要为研究筹集资金时，福克斯描述了他治疗癌症的亲身经历：
我很快意识到，这只是我追求的一半，因为当我经历了16个月令人身心俱疲的化疗折磨时，我被围绕着癌症诊所的感觉粗暴地唤醒了。有些人脸上挂着勇敢的微笑，有些人已经放弃了微笑。有一种充满希望的否认的感觉，也有一种绝望的感觉。我的追求不会是自私的。我不能离开，知道这些面孔和感觉仍然存在，即使我将从我的自由中解脱出来。在某个地方，伤害必须停止……我决心为这个事业把自己做到极限。
福克斯没有承诺他的努力会治愈癌症，但他在信的结尾写道：我们需要你的帮助。全世界癌症诊所的人都需要相信奇迹的人。我不是一个梦想家，我也不是说这将开启任何癌症的最终答案或治愈之路。我相信奇迹。我不得不这么做。癌症协会对他的献身精神表示怀疑，但同意一旦福克斯获得赞助商，并要求他获得心脏病专家的医疗证明，表明他适合尝试跑步，就会支持他。福克斯被诊断出患有左心室肥厚-心脏增大-这是一种通常与运动员有关的疾病。医生警告福克斯他面临的潜在风险，尽管他们并不认为他的病情是一个重大问题。他们支持他的参与，因为他承诺，如果他开始出现任何心脏问题，他将立即停止。[30][30]。
第二封信寄给了几家公司，寻求捐款购买一辆汽车和跑鞋，并支付跑步的其他费用。[31]福克斯还发出了其他信件，要求拨款购买一条跑步腿。他观察到，虽然他很庆幸自己在接受癌症治疗后还活着，但我记得我曾向自己承诺，如果我还活着，我会站起来，面对面地迎接这个新的挑战(为癌症研究筹资)，证明自己配得上生命，这是太多人认为理所当然的事情。福特汽车公司(Ford Motor Company)捐赠了一辆露营面包车，帝国石油公司(Imperial Oil)提供了燃料，阿迪达斯(Adidas)捐赠了他的跑鞋。[5]帝国石油公司(Imperial Oil)提供燃料，阿迪达斯(Adidas)捐赠了他的跑鞋。[32]福克斯拒绝了任何要求他为其产品代言的公司，并拒绝任何附带条件的捐赠，因为他坚持任何人都不能从他的竞选中获利。[5]。
马拉松比赛始于1980年4月12日，当时福克斯将右腿浸泡在纽芬兰和拉布拉多圣约翰附近的大西洋中，并将两个大瓶子装满了海水。他打算保留一只作为纪念品，在不列颠哥伦比亚省维多利亚结束旅程后将另一只倒进太平洋。[28]福克斯的经营得到了道格·阿尔沃德(Doug Alward)的支持，他驾驶面包车并做饭。[32][font=宋体]。
狐狸在跑步的头几天遇到了大风、暴雨和暴风雪。[2]起初，他对受到的接待感到失望，但在抵达巴斯克海峡港、纽芬兰和拉布拉多后，他感到振奋，镇上的1万名居民向他捐赠了1万多美元。[32]在整个旅程中，福克斯经常向那些他认为阻碍跑步的人表达他的愤怒和沮丧，他还经常与阿尔沃德打架。当他们到达新斯科舍省时，他们几乎没有说话的关系，福克斯当时17岁的弟弟达雷尔被安排加入他们的行列，作为缓冲。[27]福克斯于6月10日离开马里泰斯，进入魁北克面临新的挑战，因为他的团队不会说法语[33]，而且司机不断迫使他下路。[34]福克斯于6月22日抵达蒙特利尔，这是他8000公里(5000英里)旅程的三分之一，他已经筹集了超过20万美元的捐款。[24]大约在这个时候，福克斯的长跑引起了伊萨多·夏普的注意，夏普是四季酒店及度假村集团的创始人兼首席执行官，1978年，就在特里被诊断出患有黑色素瘤一年后，夏普失去了一个儿子。[35]夏普对一个独腿孩子试图完成不可能完成的事情并在全国各地奔跑的故事很感兴趣，所以他在途中向他的酒店提供了食物和住宿。当福克斯因为捐款太少而灰心丧气时，夏普(Sharp)承诺每英里2美元，并说服了近1000家其他公司也这样做。[36]夏普的鼓励说服了福克斯继续参加希望马拉松。加拿大癌症协会确信抵达渥太华参加加拿大日将有助于筹款工作，他在蒙特利尔多呆了几天。[34][34]。
6月的最后一个星期六，福克斯在霍克斯伯里镇越境进入安大略省。迎接他的是一支铜管乐队和数千名居民，他们站在街道两旁为他加油，而安大略省警察则在全省各地护送他。[37]尽管夏日酷暑难耐，他仍坚持每天跑26英里(42公里)。[33]福克斯抵达渥太华后会见了总督埃德·施莱尔和总理皮埃尔·特鲁多，并作为嘉宾出席了该市多项体育赛事。[37]在16000名球迷面前，他在加拿大足球联盟的一场比赛中进行了开球仪式，并获得了起立鼓掌。福克斯的日记反映出，当他开始理解加拿大人对他的努力有多深的感动时，他对自己受到的接待越来越兴奋。[38][38]。
7月11日，一万人在多伦多会见了福克斯，他在内森·菲利普斯广场(Nathan Phillips Square)受到了表彰。[39]AS
尽管他有极强的恢复能力，[48]福克斯还是发现，到了8月下旬，他还没开始一天的跑步，就已经筋疲力尽了。[49]9月1日，在桑德湾郊外，在经历了剧烈咳嗽和胸部疼痛后，他被迫短暂停了下来。不知道该怎么办，他继续跑，因为高速公路上的人群高喊着鼓励他。[50]几英里后，他气喘吁吁，胸口持续疼痛，要求阿尔沃德开车送他去医院。他立刻担心自己跑完了最后一公里。[51]第二天，福克斯举行了一个含泪的新闻发布会，在会上他宣布他的癌症复发并扩散到他的肺部。143天和5373公里(3339英里)后，他被迫结束了跑步。[52]福克斯拒绝了代替他跑完全程的提议，声称他想自己完成马拉松。[3]。
到福克斯被迫放弃马拉松时，他已经筹集了170万美元(相当于2018年的500万美元)。他意识到这个国家即将看到这种疾病的后果，并希望这能带来更大的慷慨。[53]在他跑步结束一周后，CTV电视网组织了一场全国性的电视伦理活动，以支持福克斯和加拿大癌症协会。[54]在加拿大和国际名人的支持下，这场长达5个小时的活动筹集了1050万美元(相当于2018年的3200万美元)。[3]在这些捐款中，不列颠哥伦比亚省和安大略省政府各捐赠100万美元，前者以福克斯的名义创建一个新的研究所，后者是给安大略省癌症治疗和研究基金会的捐赠。[55]整个冬季都在继续捐款，到次年4月，已筹集超过2300万美元(相当于2018年的6200万美元)。[56][56]。
来自世界各地的支持者和祝福者用信件和支持的象征淹没了福克斯。有一次，他收到的邮件比高贵林港其他地方的总和还要多。[57]他的名气如此之大，以至于一封写给加拿大特里·福克斯的信成功投递。[58][58]。
1980年9月，他被任命为加拿大勋章(Companion Of Canada Order)的同伴，参加了一个特殊的仪式；他是获得这一殊荣的最年轻的人。[59]不列颠哥伦比亚省副省长任命他为道格伍德勋章，该省的最高奖项。[61]加拿大体育名人堂委托举办了一个永久性的展览，福克斯被评为1980年卢马什奖的获得者，成为该国最优秀的运动员。[63]他被评为加拿大1980年年度新闻人物。“渥太华市民报”将全国对他马拉松比赛的反应描述为加拿大历史上最强烈的情感和慷慨的流露之一。[64][64]。
在接下来的几个月里，福克斯接受了多次化疗，但疾病继续蔓延。[65]随着他病情的恶化，加拿大人希望出现奇迹，教皇约翰·保罗二世发来电报说他在为福克斯祈祷。[66]医生转向实验性干扰素治疗，尽管它们对骨源性肉瘤的有效性尚不清楚。[67]他的第一次治疗出现不良反应，[68]但在休息一段时间后继续进行治疗。[69][font=宋体]。
1981年6月19日，福克斯因胸部充血和发育不良再次住进新威斯敏斯特的哥伦比亚皇家医院。
.

Jump to navigation  Jump to search  Terrance Stanley Fox   CC  OD (July 28, 1958 – June 28, 1981) was a Canadian athlete, humanitarian, and  cancer research activist. In 1980, with one leg having been amputated due to cancer, he embarked on an east to west cross-Canada run to raise money and awareness for cancer research. Although the spread of his cancer eventually forced him to end his quest after 143 days and 5,373 kilometres (3,339 mi), and ultimately cost him his life, his efforts resulted in a lasting, worldwide legacy. The annual  Terry Fox Run, first held in 1981, has grown to involve millions of participants in over 60 countries and is now the world's largest one-day fundraiser for cancer research; over  C$750 million has been raised in his name, as of January 2018.  [1]
 Fox was a distance runner and  basketball player for his  Port Coquitlam  high school, now named after him, and  Simon Fraser University. His right leg was amputated in 1977 after he was diagnosed with  osteosarcoma, though he continued to run using an artificial leg. He also played  wheelchair basketball in Vancouver, winning three national championships.
 In 1980, he began the Marathon of Hope, a cross-country run to raise money for cancer research. He hoped to raise one dollar from each of Canada's 24 million people. He began with little fanfare from  St John's, Newfoundland and Labrador, in April and ran the equivalent of a full  marathon every day. Fox had become a national star by the time he reached  Ontario; he made numerous public appearances with businessmen, athletes, and politicians in his efforts to raise money. He was forced to end his run outside  Thunder Bay when the cancer spread to his lungs. His hopes of overcoming the disease and completing his marathon ended when he died nine months later.
 In addition to being the youngest person ever named a Companion of the  Order of Canada, Fox won the 1980  Lou Marsh Award as the nation's top sportsman and was named  Canada's Newsmaker of the Year in both 1980 and 1981. Considered a national hero, he has had many buildings, statues, roads, and parks named in his honour across the country.
   Terry Fox was born on July 28, 1958, in  Winnipeg, Manitoba, to Rolland and  Betty Fox. Rolland was a switchman for the  Canadian National Railway.  [2] Fox had an elder brother, Fred, a younger brother, Darrell, and a younger sister, Judith.  [3] Fox's maternal grandmother is  Métis and Fox's younger brother Darrell has official Métis status.  [4]
 His family moved to  Surrey, British Columbia, in 1966, then settled in  Port Coquitlam, in 1968.  [3] His parents were dedicated to their family, and his mother was especially protective of her children; it was through her that Fox developed his stubborn dedication to whatever task he committed to do.  [5] His father recalled that he was extremely competitive, noting that Fox hated to lose so much that he would continue at any activity until he succeeded.  [6]
 He was an enthusiastic athlete, playing soccer,  rugby and  baseball as a child.  [7] His passion was for  basketball and though he stood only five feet tall and was a poor player at the time, Fox sought to make his school team in grade eight. Bob McGill,  [8]  [9] Fox's physical education teacher and basketball coach at Mary Hill Junior High School, felt he was better suited to be a distance runner and encouraged him to take up the sport. Fox had no desire for cross-country running, but took it up because he respected and wanted to please his coach.  [10] He was determined to continue playing basketball, even if he was the last substitute on the team. Fox played only one minute in his grade-eight season but dedicated his summers to improving his play. He became a regular player in grade nine and earned a starting position in grade ten.  [11] In grade 12, he won his high school's athlete of the year award jointly with his best friend Doug Alward.  [3]
 Though he was initially unsure whether he wanted to go to university, Fox's mother convinced him to enrol at  Simon Fraser University, where he studied  kinesiology as a stepping stone to becoming a physical education teacher.  [12] He tried out for the junior varsity basketball team, earning a spot ahead of more talented players due to his determination.  [3]
  On November 12, 1976, as Fox was driving to the family home at Morrill Street in Port Coquitlam, he became distracted by nearby bridge construction, and crashed into the back of a pickup truck. While his car was left undriveable, Fox emerged with only a sore right knee. He again felt pain in December, but chose to ignore it until the end of basketball season.  [13] By March 1977, the pain had intensified and he finally went to a hospital, where he was diagnosed with  osteosarcoma, a form of cancer that often starts near the knees.  [3] Fox believed his car accident weakened his knee and left it vulnerable to the disease, though his doctors argued there was no connection.  [14] He was told that his leg had to be amputated, he would require  chemotherapy treatment, and that recent medical advances meant he had a 50-percent chance of survival. Fox learned that two years before, the figure would have been only 15 percent; the improvement in survival rates impressed on him the value of cancer research.  [15]
 With the help of an artificial leg, Fox was walking three weeks after the amputation.  [3] He then progressed to playing golf with his father.  [16] Doctors were impressed with Fox's positive outlook, stating it contributed to his rapid recovery.  [17] He endured sixteen months of chemotherapy and found the time he spent in the British Columbia Cancer Control Agency facility difficult as he watched fellow cancer patients suffer and die from the disease.  [18] Fox ended his treatment with new purpose: he felt he owed his survival to medical advances and wished to live his life in a way that would help others find courage.  [19]
 In the summer of 1977,  Rick Hansen, working with the Canadian Wheelchair Sports Association, invited Fox to try out for his  wheelchair basketball team.  [20] Although he was undergoing chemotherapy treatments at the time, Fox's energy impressed Hansen.  [3] Less than two months after learning how to play the sport, Fox was named a member of the team for the national championship in Edmonton.  [21] He won three national titles with the team,  [3] and was named an all-star by the North American Wheelchair Basketball Association in 1980.  [22]
   The night before his cancer surgery, Fox had been given an article about  Dick Traum, the first amputee to complete the  New York City Marathon.  [3] The article inspired him; he embarked on a 14-month training program, telling his family he planned to compete in a marathon himself.  [2] In private, he devised a more extensive plan. His hospital experiences had made Fox angry at how little money was dedicated to cancer research. He intended to run the length of Canada in the hope of increasing cancer awareness, a goal he initially divulged to only his friend Douglas Alward.  [23]
 Fox ran with an unusual gait, as he was required to hop-step on his good leg due to the extra time the springs in his artificial leg required to reset after each step.  [24] He found the training painful as the additional pressure he had to place on both his good leg and his stump led to bone bruises, blisters and intense pain. Fox found that after about 20 minutes of each run, he crossed a pain threshold and the run became easier.  [25]
 On September 2, 1979, Fox competed in a 17-mile (27 km) road race in  Prince George. He finished in last place, ten minutes behind his closest competitor, but his effort was met with tears and applause from the other participants.  [3] Following the marathon, he revealed his full plan to his family.  [26] His mother discouraged him, angering Fox, though she later came to support the project. She recalled, "He said, 'I thought you'd be one of the first persons to believe in me.' And I wasn't. I was the first person who let him down".  [27] Fox initially hoped to raise $1 million,  [27] then $10 million, but later sought to raise $1 for each of Canada's 24 million citizens.  [28]
  On October 15, 1979, Fox sent a letter to the  Canadian Cancer Society in which he announced his goal and appealed for funding. He stated that he would "conquer" his disability, and promised to complete his run, even if he had to "crawl every last mile". Explaining why he wanted to raise money for research, Fox described his personal experience of cancer treatment:
 I soon realized that that would only be half my quest, for as I went through the 16 months of the physically and emotionally draining ordeal of chemotherapy, I was rudely awakened by the feelings that surrounded and coursed through the cancer clinic. There were faces with the brave smiles, and the ones who had given up smiling. There were feelings of hopeful denial, and the feelings of despair. My quest would not be a selfish one. I could not leave knowing these faces and feelings would still exist, even though I would be set free from mine. Somewhere the hurting must stop....and I was determined to take myself to the limit for this cause.  [29]
 Fox made no promises that his efforts would lead to a cure for cancer, but he closed his letter with the statement: "We need your help. The people in cancer clinics all over the world need people who believe in miracles. I am not a dreamer, and I am not saying that this will initiate any kind of definitive answer or cure to cancer. I believe in miracles. I have to."  [29] The Cancer Society was skeptical of his dedication, but agreed to support Fox once he had acquired sponsors and requested he get a medical certificate from a heart specialist stating that he was fit to attempt the run. Fox was diagnosed with  left ventricular hypertrophy – an enlarged heart – a condition commonly associated with athletes. Doctors warned Fox of the potential risks he faced, though they did not consider his condition a significant concern. They endorsed his participation when he promised that he would stop immediately if he began to experience any heart problems.  [30]
 A second letter was sent to several corporations seeking donations for a vehicle and running shoes, and to cover the other costs of the run.  [31] Fox sent other letters asking for grants to buy a running leg. He observed that while he was grateful to be alive following his cancer treatment, "I remember promising myself that, should I live, I would rise up to meet this new challenge [of fundraising for cancer research] face to face and prove myself worthy of life, something too many people take for granted."  [31] The  Ford Motor Company donated a camper van,  [5] while  Imperial Oil contributed fuel, and  Adidas his running shoes.  [32] Fox turned away any company that requested he endorse their products and refused any donation that carried conditions, as he insisted that nobody was to profit from his run.  [5]
   The Marathon began on April 12, 1980, when Fox dipped his right leg in the Atlantic Ocean near  St John's, Newfoundland and Labrador, and filled two large bottles with ocean water. He intended to keep one as a souvenir and pour the other into the Pacific Ocean upon completing his journey at  Victoria, British Columbia.  [28] Fox was supported on his run by Doug Alward, who drove the van and cooked meals.  [32]
 Fox was met with gale-force winds, heavy rain and a snowstorm in the first days of his run.  [2] He was initially disappointed with the reception he received, but was heartened upon arriving in  Channel-Port aux Basques, Newfoundland and Labrador, where the town's 10,000 residents presented him with a donation of over $10,000.  [32] Throughout the trip, Fox frequently expressed his anger and frustration to those he saw as impeding the run, and he fought regularly with Alward. By the time they reached  Nova Scotia, they were barely on speaking terms, and it was arranged for Fox's brother Darrell, then 17, to join them as a buffer.  [27] Fox left the  Maritimes on June 10 and faced new challenges entering  Quebec due to his group's inability to speak French  [33] and drivers who continually forced him off the road.  [34] Fox arrived in  Montreal on June 22, one-third of the way through his 8,000-kilometre (5,000 mi) journey, having collected over $200,000 in donations.  [24] Around this time, Fox's run caught the attention of  Isadore Sharp who was the founder and CEO of  Four Seasons Hotels and Resorts – and who had lost a son to melanoma in 1978 just a year after Terry's diagnosis.  [35] Sharp was intrigued by the story of a one-legged kid "trying to do the impossible" and run across the country; so he offered food and accommodation at his hotels en route. When Fox was discouraged because so few people were making donations, Sharp pledged $2 a mile [to the run] and persuaded close to 1,000 other corporations to do the same.  [36] Sharp's encouragement persuaded Fox to continue with the Marathon of Hope. Convinced by the Canadian Cancer Society that arriving in  Ottawa for  Canada Day would aid fundraising efforts, he remained in Montreal for a few extra days.  [34]
  Fox crossed into Ontario at the town of  Hawkesbury on the last Saturday in June. He was met by a brass band and thousands of residents who lined the streets to cheer him on, while the  Ontario Provincial Police gave him an escort throughout the province.  [37] Despite the sweltering heat of summer, he continued to run 26 miles (42 km) per day.  [33] On his arrival in  Ottawa, Fox met Governor General  Ed Schreyer and Prime Minister  Pierre Trudeau and was the guest of honour at numerous sporting events in the city.  [37] In front of 16,000 fans, he performed a ceremonial kickoff at a  Canadian Football League game and was given a standing ovation. Fox's journal reflected his growing excitement at the reception he had received as he began to understand how deeply moved Canadians were by his efforts.  [38]
 On July 11, a crowd of 10,000 people met Fox in  Toronto, where he was honoured in  Nathan Phillips Square.  [39] As he ran to the square, he was joined on the road by many people, including  National Hockey League star  Darryl Sittler, who presented Fox with his 1980 All-Star Game jersey. The Cancer Society estimated it collected $100,000 in donations that day alone.  [3] That evening he threw the  ceremonial first pitch at  Exhibition Stadium preceding a baseball game between the  Toronto Blue Jays and the  Cleveland Indians. As he continued through southern Ontario, he was met by  Hockey Hall of Famer  Bobby Orr who presented him with a cheque for $25,000. Fox considered meeting Orr the highlight of his journey.  [3]
  As Fox's fame grew, the Cancer Society scheduled him to attend more functions and give more speeches.  [40] Fox attempted to accommodate any request that he believed would raise money, no matter how far out of his way it took him.  [41] He bristled, however, at what he felt were media intrusions into his personal life, for example when the   Toronto Star reported that he had gone on a date.  [42] Fox was left unsure whom he could trust in the media after negative articles began to emerge, including one by   The Globe and Mail that characterized him as a "tyrannical brother" who verbally abused Darrell and claimed he was running because he held a grudge against a doctor who had misdiagnosed his condition, allegations he referred to as "trash".  [43]
 The physical demands of running a marathon every day took their toll on Fox's body. Apart from the rest days in Montreal taken at the request of the Cancer Society, he refused to take a day off, even on his 22nd birthday.  [44] He frequently suffered shin splints and an inflamed knee. He developed cysts on his stump and experienced dizzy spells.  [45] At one point, he suffered a soreness in his ankle that would not go away. Although he feared he had developed a stress fracture, he ran for three more days before seeking medical attention, and was then relieved to learn it was  tendonitis and could be treated with painkillers.  [46] Fox rejected calls for him to seek regular medical checkups,  [47] and dismissed suggestions he was risking his future health.  [43]
 In spite of his immense recuperative capacity,  [48] Fox found that by late August he was exhausted before he began his day's run.  [49] On September 1, outside Thunder Bay, he was forced to stop briefly after he suffered an intense coughing fit and experienced pains in his chest. Unsure what to do, he resumed running as the crowds along the highway shouted out their encouragement.  [50] A few miles later, short of breath and with continued chest pain, he asked Alward to drive him to a hospital. He feared immediately that he had run his last kilometre.  [51] The next day, Fox held a tearful press conference during which he announced that his cancer had returned and spread to his lungs. He was forced to end his run after 143 days and 5,373 kilometres (3,339 mi).  [52] Fox refused offers to complete the run in his stead, stating that he wanted to complete his marathon himself.  [3]
   Fox had raised $1.7 million (equivalent to $5 million in 2018) by the time he was forced to abandon the Marathon. He realized that the nation was about to see the consequences of the disease, and hoped that this might lead to greater generosity.  [53] A week after his run ended, the  CTV Television Network organized a nationwide  telethon in support of Fox and the Canadian Cancer Society.  [54] Supported by Canadian and international celebrities, the five-hour event raised $10.5 million (equivalent to $32 million in 2018).  [3] Among the donations were $1 million each by the governments of British Columbia and Ontario, the former to create a new research institute to be founded in Fox's name, and the latter an endowment given to the Ontario Cancer Treatment and Research Foundation.  [55] Donations continued throughout the winter, and by the following April, over $23 million had been raised (equivalent to $62 million in 2018).  [56]
 Supporters and well wishers from around the world inundated Fox with letters and tokens of support. At one point, he was receiving more mail than the rest of Port Coquitlam combined.  [57] Such was his fame that one letter addressed simply to "Terry Fox, Canada" was successfully delivered.  [58]
 In September 1980, he was invested in a special ceremony as a Companion of the  Order of Canada; he was the youngest person to be so honoured.  [59]  [60] The  Lieutenant Governor of British Columbia named him to the  Order of the Dogwood, the province's highest award.  [61]  Canada's Sports Hall of Fame commissioned a permanent exhibit,  [62] and Fox was named the winner of the  Lou Marsh Award for 1980 as the nation's top athlete.  [63] He was named Canada's 1980  Newsmaker of the Year. The   Ottawa Citizen described the national response to his marathon as "one of the most powerful outpourings of emotion and generosity in Canada's history".  [64]
  In the following months, Fox received multiple chemotherapy treatments, but the disease continued to spread.  [65] As his condition worsened, Canadians hoped for a miracle and  Pope John Paul II sent a telegram saying that he was praying for Fox.  [66] Doctors turned to experimental  interferon treatments, though their effectiveness against  osteogenic sarcoma was unknown.  [67] He suffered an adverse reaction to his first treatment,  [68] but continued the program after a period of rest.  [69]
 Fox was re-admitted to the  Royal Columbian Hospital in  New Westminster on June 19, 1981, with chest congestion and develo

note:
2018年，当DARPA为有争议的EmDrive提供资金时，这看起来像是一场豪赌。许多物理学家对这次革命性的太空运动不屑一顾，认为这只是一门伪科学。DARPA在今年2月告诉大众机械公司，现在它的EmDrive项目已经为第二阶段开了绿灯。与此同时，其他团队希望在今年晚些时候完成这项技术的最终演示。
英国普利茅斯大学地球测量学讲师、美国国防部高级研究计划局(DARPA)EmDrive项目负责人迈克·麦卡洛克(Mike McCulloch)表示，这是一项可以改变太空旅行的技术，可以看到飞船悄悄地从发射台上升空，并延伸到太阳系之外。“我们还可以在(漫长的)人类寿命内，90年后，向半人比邻星发射一艘无人探测器。”
🌌，你喜欢坏的太空玩意儿。我们也是。让我们这些书呆子一起在宇宙中穿梭吧。
“基于理论模型的性能预测导致了新的推进器设计，这些新的设计可能有助于为未来的开发和测试活动提供信息，”DARPA的一位发言人告诉“大众机械”(Popular Mechanical)。
随着两项正在进行的研究严格测试EmDrive的“不可能性”，这一围绕着天文工程界20多年的有争议的驱动力距离生死存亡的时刻只有几个月的时间了。
最初的EmDrive由罗杰·肖耶(Roger Shawyer)于1998年建造，是一个密封的铜管(如上图所示)，一端比另一端更宽。根据Shawyer的说法，如果你在管子内反射微波，它们在一个方向上比在另一个方向上施加更大的力，在不需要任何推进剂的情况下产生净推力。
根据传统物理学，这是不应该发生的。简而言之，像拟议的EmDrive这样的封闭系统应该不会产生推力。但包括NASA的Eagleworks(正式名称为先进物理推进实验室，旨在探索新技术)和中国西安的一个团队在内的几个研究小组进行了尝试，并获得了相同的结果，即一个小但不同的净力量。
批评家们认为实验结果肯定是错的，而麦卡洛克则认为我们只是还不了解物理学。
麦卡洛克发展了量化惯性(QI)理论，解释了这种效应以及它如何帮助人类进行太空旅行。麦卡洛克在过去18个月的大部分时间里一直在磨练这一理论，并在实验室检查其预测与结果如何匹配。
西班牙马德里的何塞·路易斯·佩雷斯·迪亚兹(Jose Luis Perez Diaz)和德国德累斯顿的马丁·塔马尔(Martin Tajmar)正在进行该项目的实验部分。Tajmar证实，他计划在2021年2月发表两篇论文，一篇是关于“普通”微波EMDrives的，另一篇是关于基于激光的EMDrives的。在实验方面，Tajmar仍在努力消除所有可能的误差源。
“我们仍在改善我们的余额，测试仍在继续，”Tajmar说。“尤其是，我们正在努力进一步减少磁场与环境的相互作用，这是我们在之前的测试中发现的主要副作用。”
当被问及他是否可能对之前的测试中看到的明显推力有其他解释时，塔伊马尔只说“等待论文…”。
麦卡洛克的预测之一是，推力既可以用光，也可以用微波。迪亚兹在马德里进行了一项实验，用2000次缠绕的不对称光纤环来验证这一假设，他称之为光子环。
与微波版本一样，不对称性才是最重要的。赛车经历不同的力取决于它绕过的弯道有多大，根据QI的说法，光线绕过不同曲率的弯道也会产生类似的效果。迪亚兹发现，通过光子环传递激光脉冲会产生足够的净力来移动它。
在迪亚兹建造和测试了这个仪器后，他把它送到了塔伊马尔位于德累斯顿的实验室，对推力进行了验证和精确测量，今年夏天正在进行这项工作。麦卡洛克说，推力似乎在1到4微牛顿之间-恰好是他的理论预测的量。
这种设备的优点是，光纤比需要精密加工的铜腔便宜得多，安装和测试也容易得多。一旦设计完善，世界各地的物理实验室应该可以制作自己的光子环路，并亲眼看到效果。
最大的挑战是准确测量如此低的推力水平，因为实验误差可能会严重扭曲结果。麦卡洛克认为，这一问题可以在下一阶段得到解决。通过将推力方程输入优化软件，McCulloch设计了一种比Shawyer最初的驱动器效率高数千倍的驱动器。这种设计的一端有一系列的尖刺--麦卡洛克说，他们以巴特·辛普森的尖尖头发的名字给它起了个绰号--巴特·辛普森(Bart Simpson)。
目前的设计计算出，1千瓦功率的推力约为0.012牛顿(相当于回形针或葡萄干的重量)，类似于用于操纵卫星的驱动器。但麦卡洛克相信，他们至少可以做得更好几倍，实验将证明这一点。
“巴特驱动器应该能提供0.012 N/kW的功率，与工业离子驱动器相当，但没有推进剂，这使得它们很重，寿命有限，”麦卡洛克说。“如果我们能在不需要推进剂的情况下提供0.1N/kW(大约是现有推进器的8倍)，那么卫星公司会非常感兴趣，我希望这是让这项技术腾飞的门槛。”
此内容是从YouTube导入的。您可以在他们的网站上找到不同格式的相同内容，也可以找到更多信息。
比现有的太空驱动器产生更多推力的工作EmDrive将是革命性的，但McCulLouch的QI理论在物理学上是不被接受的-甚至不是很接近。
症结在于动量守恒定律，即在封闭系统内动量保持不变。这就是为什么你实际上不能靠你的靴子把自己拉起来的原因。这一结果在过去的一个世纪里一直是坚实的，并为绝大多数科学家所接受。
这就是为什么大多数评论家不把EmDrive当真，一些人嘲笑它，说“魔法太空独角兽”提供推力，或者说“EmDrive，更像BS Drive”。科学的重量在他们这边，需要一些相当戏剧性的东西来取代它。
康涅狄格大学先进推进系统专家Brice Cassenti同意普遍观点，认为EmDrive似乎不可信，因为它违反了动量守恒定律。
卡森蒂告诉Popular Mechanical：“只有锥形天线发出的电磁波才能提供动量变化，才能提供力，而力太小了几个数量级。”他认为，EmDrive在DARPA中的经久不衰的人气是因为它提供了承诺。
卡森蒂说：“许多热心的人愿意相信，这是一种可以用来摆脱已知物理原理对空间推进系统的限制，并让人类向恒星航行敞开大门的方法。”
卡森蒂认为，探索新的和具有挑战性的概念是重要的，但考虑到提供的无限多样性，他更喜欢那些有已知物理学支持的概念。
卡森蒂说：“在我看来，将工程努力投资于满足已知物理原理的现有途径，并逐步在新的途径上建立科学供工程师探索，总是更好的。”
不管是真是假，DARPA并不是唯一一个在研究这项技术的机构。
麦卡洛克还在与加利福尼亚州的一个财团洽谈，这些财团正在创建自己的地平线驾驶实验。这个组织包括南加州大学(University Of Southern California)和一个组织，他只允许将该组织描述为一家“大型航空公司”。他们的目标是“发自内心”地演示这种效果，这样，与微推力演示不同，任何看到它的人都会相信。麦卡洛克说，结果应该在本月公布。
此内容是从{embed-name}导入的。您可以在他们的网站上找到不同格式的相同内容，也可以找到更多信息。
就连美国海军也一直在涉足这一领域。他们的研究人员之一，塞尔瓦托·塞萨尔·派斯，最近为一种带有锥形谐振腔和微波发射器的太空驱动器申请了一项专利，该发射器与EmDrive非常相似。海军拒绝讨论这项工作。
也有其他的玩家。21世纪初，来自西安西北工业大学的中国研究人员是最早对肖耶的工作感兴趣的人，他们发表了一些科学论文，概述了他们显然进展迅速的情况。有一系列媒体表示，他们正准备在2016年在太空测试他们的驱动器，但自那以后，一切都保持沉默。
DARPA项目的当前阶段将持续到2021年5月，除非有切实的结果，否则该项目可能会以失败告终。二十年后，对EmDrive的最终裁决即将到来。

note_en:
When DARPA   put money behind the controversial EmDrive in 2018, it looked like a big gamble. Many physicists had dismissed the revolutionary space drive as simply fake science. Now its EmDrive project is greenlit for Phase 2, DARPA told  Popular Mechanics in February this year. Meanwhile, other teams are hoping to reach a final demonstration of the technology later this year.
 "This is a technology which could transform space travel and see craft lifting silently off from launchpads and reaching beyond the solar system," says Mike McCulloch, a lecturer in geomatics at the University of Plymouth, U.K., and leader behind DARPA’s EmDrive project. “We can also get an unmanned probe to Proxima Centauri in a (long) human lifetime, 90 years.”
 🌌  You like badass space stuff. So do we.  Let's nerd out over the universe together.
  “Theoretical model-based predictions of performance have led to new thruster designs, and these new designs may help inform future development and testing activities,” a DARPA spokesman told  Popular Mechanics.
 With two ongoing studies rigorously testing the EmDrive’s “impossibility,” the controversial drive that’s hung around astro-engineering circles for more than two decades is only months away from its do-or-die moment.
   The original EmDrive, built by Roger Shawyer in 1998, is a sealed copper tube (pictured above) wider at one end than the other. According to Shawyer, if you bounce microwaves around inside the tube, they exert more force in one direction than the other, creating a net thrust without the need for any propellant.
  According to conventional physics, this shouldn’t happen. Put simply, closed systems like the proposed EmDrive should not generate thrust. But several research groups, including NASA’s Eagleworks (formally known as the   Advanced Physics Propulsion Laboratory, set up to explore new technologies) and a team at Xi’an in China, tried it and got the same result, a small-but-distinct net force.
 Critics believe that the experimental results must be wrong while McCulloch believes we just don't understand the physics yet.
  McCulloch has developed a theory of Quantized Inertia (QI), which explains the effect and how it could help with human space travel. McCulloch has spent much of the past 18 months honing this theory and checking how its predictions match results in the laboratory.
 Jose Luis Perez Diaz in Madrid, Spain, and Martin Tajmar in Dresden, Germany, are carrying out the experimental side of the project. Tajmar confirms that he plans to publish two papers in February 2021, one on the “normal” microwave EmDrives and the other on the laser-based EmDrives. On the experimental side, Tajmar is still working on eliminating every possible source of error.
 “We are still improving our balances and testing continues, “ says Tajmar. “In particular we are working on further reduction of magnetic field interactions with the environment, which was the major side-effect that we discovered in previous testing.”
 When asked whether he might have an alternative explanation for the apparent thrust seen in previous tests, Tajmar only says to “Wait for the papers…”
  One of McCulloch’s predictions is that thrust can be achieved with light as well as microwaves. Diaz has carried out experiments in Madrid testing this hypothesis by using an asymmetric loop of optical fiber wound 2,000 times, which he calls a Photon Loop.
 As with the microwave version, the asymmetry is what matters. A racing car experiences different forces depending on how sharp a bend it goes around, and according to QI, a similar effect occurs with light going around bends of different curvature. Diaz found that passing laser pulses through the Photon Loop produces enough net force to move it.
 After Diaz constructed and tested this apparatus, he sent it to Tajmar’s laboratory in Dresden for verification and precise measurement of the thrust, which is being carried out this summer. McCulloch says that the thrust appears to be between one and four micronewtons—exactly the amount his theory predicts.
  The advantage of this apparatus is that fiber optics are much cheaper and easier to set up and test than copper cavities requiring precise machining. Once the design is perfected, it should be possible for physics labs around the world to make their own Photon Loops and see the effect for themselves.
 The big challenge is accurately measuring such a low level of thrust as an experimental error can skew results dramatically. McCulloch believes this can be resolved in the next phase. By feeding the thrust equations into optimization software, McCulloch has designed a drive thousands of times more efficient than Shawyer’s original. This design has a series of spikes at one end—McCulloch says they nicknamed it the Bart Drive after Bart Simpson’s spiky hair.
 The current design is calculated to provide a thrust of about .012 Newtons (equal to the weight of a paperclip, or a raisin) for one kilowatt of power, similar to the drives used to maneuver satellites. But McCulloch believes they can do several times better at least, and that the experiments will prove this.
 “The Bart Drive should provide 0.012 N/kW, comparable to industry ion drives already but without propellant, which makes them heavy, and with a limited lifespan,” says McCulloch. “If we can provide 0.1 N/kW [about 8 times more than existing drives] without needing propellant then the satellite companies will be very interested, and my hope is that this is the threshold that will make the technology take off.”
  This content is imported from YouTube. You may be able to find the same content in another format, or you may be able to find more information, at their web site.
  A working EmDrive which produces more thrust than existing space drives would be revolutionary, but McCullouch’s QI theory is  not accepted physics—not even close.
 The sticking point is the law of conservation of momentum, which says that inside a closed system momentum remains constant. This is why you cannot actually pull yourself up by your booststaps. This result has remained solid for the past century and is accepted by the vast majority of scientists.
 This is why most critics don't take the EmDrive seriously, and some mock it with comments about ‘  magic space unicorns’ providing thrust, or ‘  EmDrive, more like BS Drive.’ The weight of science is on their side, and it will take something pretty dramatic to displace it.
    Brice Cassenti, an expert in advanced propulsion systems at the University of Connecticut, goes with the general view that the EmDrive does not seem plausible because it violates the law of conservation of momentum.
 “Only the electromagnetic waves emitted by the conical antenna can provide a momentum change, that can provide a force, and the force is several orders of magnitude too small,” Cassenti told  Popular Mechanics. He believes the EmDrive’s enduring popularity with DARPA is because of the promise it offers.
 “Many enthusiastic individuals want to believe it is a method that can be used to escape the constraints of known physical principles on space propulsion systems and open up humanity to voyages to the stars,” says Cassenti.
 Cassenti believes it is important to explore new and challenging concepts, but given the infinite variety on offer, he prefers those with the backing of known physics.
 “In my opinion, it will always be better to invest engineering efforts in existing avenues that satisfy known physical principles and gradually build up science in new avenues for engineers to explore,” says Cassenti.
  Whether real or not, DARPA isn’t the only one working on this technology.
 McCulloch is also talking to a consortium in California who are creating their own Horizon Drive experiment. This group includes the University of Southern California and an organization, which he is only allowed to describe as a ‘major aerospace company.’ Their goal is to demonstrate the effect “viscerally” so that, unlike micro-thrust demonstration, anyone who sees it will believe. McCulloch says that results should be released this month.
 This content is imported from {embed-name}. You may be able to find the same content in another format, or you may be able to find more information, at their web site.
  Even the U.S. Navy has been dabbling in this area. One of their researchers, Salvatore Cesar Pais, recently took out a patent for a space drive with a conical resonant cavity and microwave emitters remarkably similar to the EmDrive. The Navy declined to discuss this work.
 There are other players, too. Chinese researchers from Northwestern Polytechnical University in Xi’an were the earliest to take an interest in Shawyer’s work in the early 2000s, publishing a   number of scientific papers outlining their apparently rapid progress. There was a flurry of media indicating they were preparing to test their drive in space in 2016, but everything has been silent since.
 The current phase of the DARPA project will continue until May 2021, and it’s possible that the project will fizzle out unless there are tangible results. After two decades, the final verdict on the EmDrive is quickly approaching.

还有其他Python实现，每个实现都有不同的侧重点。像CPython一样，他们想要做的事情总是比开发人员要做的要多。可能会感兴趣的一些主要示例包括：
IronPython：一个专注于与.NET和Mono提供的公共语言运行时(CLR)良好集成的Python解释器。
Stackless：一个Python解释器，专注于提供轻量级微线程，同时在很大程度上与CPython特定的扩展模块兼容。
任何人都可以克隆本指南的源代码。请参阅帮助开发人员手册。
请注意，Python Software Foundation支持的框架上的所有交互都包含在PSF行为准则中，其中包括Python本身开发中使用的所有基础设施(例如，邮件列表、问题跟踪器、GitHub等)。一般来说，这意味着每个人都应该是开放、体贴和尊重他人的，无论他们在项目中处于什么位置。
note_en:
This guide is a comprehensive resource for   contributingto  Python – for both new and experienced contributors. It is  maintained by the samecommunity that maintains Python. We welcome your contributions to Python!
 Here are the basic steps needed to get   set up and contribute apatch. This is meant as a checklist, once you know the basics. For completeinstructions please see the   setup guide.
 Install and set up   Git and other dependencies(see the   Git Setup page for detailed information).
  See also   more detailed instructions,  how to install and build dependencies,and the platform-specific pages for   UNIX,  Mac OS, and   Windows.
 On   most Mac OS X systems, replace   ./pythonwith   ./python.exe. On Windows, use   python.bat. With Python2.7, replace   test with   test.regrtest.
 If an issue does not already exist, please  create it. Trivial issues (e.g. typo fixes) do notrequire any issue to be created.
 Once you fixed the issue, run the tests, run   make  patchcheck, and ifeverything is ok, commit.
 Push the branch on your fork on GitHub and   create a pull request. Include the issue number using   bpo-NNNN in thepull request description. For example:
 First time contributors will need to sign the Contributor LicensingAgreement (CLA) as described in the   Licensing section ofthis guide.
  The master branch is currently the future Python 3.10, and is the onlybranch that accepts new features. The latest release for each Pythonversion can be found on the  download page.
  bugfixes and security fixes are accepted, new binaries are stillreleased. (Also called  maintenance mode or  stable release)
 only security fixes are accepted and no more binaries are released,but new source-only versions can be released
  By default, the end-of-life is scheduled 5 years after the first release,but can be adjusted by the release manager of each branch. All Python 2versions have reached end-of-life.
 We encourage everyone to contribute to Python and that’s why we have put up thisdeveloper’s guide. If you still have questions after reviewing the material inthis guide, then the  Core Python Mentorship group is available to help guide newcontributors through the process.
 A number of individuals from the Python community have contributed to a seriesof excellent guides at  Open Source Guides.
       It is  recommended that the above documents be read as needed. Newcontributors will build understanding of the CPython workflow by reading thesections mentioned in this table. Youcan stop where you feel comfortable and begin contributing immediately withoutreading and understanding these documents all at once. If you do choose to skiparound within the documentation, be aware that it is written assuming precedingdocumentation has been read so you may find it necessary to backtrack to fill inmissing concepts and terminology.
 Improving Python’s code, documentation and tests are ongoing tasks that arenever going to be “finished”, as Python operates as part of an ever-evolvingsystem of technology. An even more challenging ongoing task than thesenecessary maintenance activities is finding ways to make Python, in the form ofthe standard library and the language definition, an even better tool in adeveloper’s toolkit.
 While these kinds of change are much rarer than those described above, they dohappen and that process is also described as part of this guide:
 This guide is specifically for contributing to the Python reference interpreter,also known as CPython (while most of the standard library is written in Python,the interpreter core is written in C and integrates most easily with the C andC++ ecosystems).
 There are other Python implementations, each with a different focus. LikeCPython, they always have more things they would like to do than they havedevelopers to work on them. Some major examples that may be of interest are:
 IronPython: A Python interpreter focused on good integration with theCommon Language Runtime (CLR) provided by .NET and Mono
 Stackless: A Python interpreter focused on providing lightweightmicrothreads while remaining largely compatible with CPython specificextension modules
  Anyone can clone the sources for this guide. See  Helping with the Developer’s Guide.
 Please note that all interactions on Python Software Foundation-supportedinfrastructure is  coveredby the  PSF Code of Conduct,which includes all infrastructure used in the development of Python itself(e.g. mailing lists, issue trackers, GitHub, etc.).In general this means everyone is expected to be open, considerate, andrespectful of others no matter what their position is within the project.

有没有试过给你的手机发送推送通知？能有多难呢？当我试图在不涉及第三方的情况下推送简单的推送通知时，我感到很惊讶。这不仅仅是困难，他们使得在没有订阅他们的服务的情况下发送简单的推送通知几乎是不可能的。此外，如果你想在局域网或互联网上推送通知，那也无关紧要，你必须订阅云消息服务。
我们为什么需要云消息服务？大量的设备都在NAT后面，不会直接暴露在互联网上。在这些情况下，您需要第三方将通知推送到这些设备。NAT后的设备侦听远程服务器，该服务器可以在需要时推送通知。这是有道理的。不过，云服务提供商从未承认这个问题。因为这个问题可以通过开放的云消息传递平台轻松解决。取而代之的是，他们有一个扭曲的理由，他们不允许任何程序在后台运行，除了他们的程序，以节省电池。这使得不可能实现开放的云消息传递平台。
我想通过局域网发送推送通知。实施我自己的服务是徒劳的，因为Android积极的电池管理。我想也许亚行可以在这方面有所帮助，但令我惊讶的是，即使是亚行也不能在没有root的情况下发送推送通知。很明显，他们想把我们和他们的生态系统锁在一起，给我们提供有缺陷的电池优化推理。
嘿，谷歌，为什么不关闭位置和频繁的播放服务更新，因为它们对我的手机电池也没有帮助。这种明目张胆的伪善应该结束了。
note_en:
Ever tried to send a push notification to your phone? How hard can it be? I was surprised when I tried to push a simple push notification without involving a third party. It's not just hard they have made it near impossible to send a simple push notification without subscribing to their service. Moreover it doesn't matter if you want to push notification on your LAN or Internet, you must subscribe to cloud messaging service.
 Why do we need cloud messaging service? A large number of devices are behind NAT and are not exposed to Internet directly. In these cases, you need a third party to push the notification to these devices. The device behind NAT listens to a remote server which can push notification as and when required. This makes sense. The cloud service providers never admit to this problem though. As this problem can be easily resolved with an open cloud messaging platform. Instead, they have this warped reasoning where they don't allow any program to run in background, except theirs, "to save the battery". This makes it impossible to implement an open cloud messaging platform.
  I wanted to send a push notification over LAN. Implementing my own service was futile because of Android's aggressive battery management. I thought maybe adb can help here but to my surprise even adb can't send a push notification without being root. It's obvious that they want us to be locked down with their ecosystem and are giving us the flawed reasoning of battery optimization.
 Hey Google, why not shutdown location and frequent play service updates as they aren't helping my phone battery either. This blatant hypocrisy needs to end.

您还可以使用&#34；Lookup&#34；选项卡搜索/查找现有内容(默认为Rumin后端)。

使用屏幕截图按钮，只需单击一次即可截取屏幕截图。它捕获当前浏览器窗口中的内容。

note_en:
Browser extension for  Rumin which allows you to save learnings from online resources. ( Chrome Web Store link,  Firefox Add-on link)
 It comes with site-specific logic for automatically extracting key information, such as video playing time on YouTube, course info and reference on edX, metadata on Kindle notes etc. This can be easily extended to add extraction logic to any web page.
  You can also search / look up existing content (defaulting to the Rumin backend) using the "Lookup" tab.
    You can use the extension to bookmark a page, or select a particular passage by simply highlighting it.
 By default, the extension can be opened using the  Ctrl / Cmd + K keyboard shortcut.
  Take a screenshot in one click, using the "Screenshot" button. It captures the content in the current browser window.
  The extension comes with page-specific logic extracts the key metadata, and turns page content into structured data. This way, you don't have to copy and paste back and forth.
    The "Lookup" tab fetches existing captured notes/snippets from the current url by default. You can also use it to search your knowledge base.
  It is easy to add support for automatic extraction on more sites. All you need is logic to check the url (or content) of the current page, and add logic to update  customFields accordingly.
 For example, it can be extended to save the top Hacker News comments on a thread, or save metadata of an answer on Quora.
  To use this extension for a different project, simply swap out the getrumin.com API calls with your custom endpoints.

note:
在科技领域，假设用户愚蠢和懒惰来设计产品是一种常见的说法。我认为这既是不尊重的，也是错误的。
这个想法植根于围绕产品可用性的大量研究，但它已经被糟蹋了。认为这是对“不要让我思考”命题的歪曲。
不要让我认为，Steve Krug的开创性网络可用性书籍告诉我们，产品应该尽可能简单，供用户使用。产品不应该是不言自明的(给出一套说明书就能理解)，它们应该是不言而喻的(不需要阅读说明书就可以使用)。一扇好的门有一个推拉标志，让它不言而喻，但它仍然需要你阅读和思考。一扇更好的门甚至根本不需要这个标签-你本能地知道该怎么做。
但不知何故，我们颠覆了这个想法。我们说，用户是懒惰的，甚至是愚蠢的。他们只想用手指轻弹一个无限的馈送，让他们的眼睛从一个项目转到另一个项目。
但在“不要让我思考”一书中，克鲁格从未以贬义的方式提到过用户。他告诉我们好的产品应该如何发挥作用，以及为什么基础心理学支持这一点。人们希望尽可能地减少认知摩擦。人们不喜欢不必要的认知摩擦。人们快速浏览，“蒙混过关”产品。而且，最重要的是，人们不会做出努力，除非他们认为这是值得的。这些都是由可用性研究和心理学支持的事实。
换句话说，他告诉我们好的产品应该是什么样子，以及人们如何使用它们。但他不会对用户做出评判。这取决于我们。
因此，很自然地，我们应用我们的世界观，我们的价值观。如果你轻蔑地看待你的用户，那么人们不喜欢复杂产品背后的原因是因为他们愚蠢和懒惰。另一方面，如果你尊重你的用户，你可能会有不同的看法。
首先，通过数百万年的进化，我们的大脑已经被连接起来，以节省精力和时间。这实际上不是懒惰，而是聪明和保护我们最有价值的资产之一。当然，我们不会从事一项活动，除非我们认为它值得付出代价(尽管有一些方法可以欺骗我们，稍后会详细介绍)。如果要弄清楚一项活动需要付出多少努力，我们就会完全避免这种活动。这是我们大脑中起作用的、实用的部分。
其次，我们是复杂的情感捆绑。即使我们很聪明，我们也不喜欢感觉愚蠢。而复杂、困难的事情会让我们觉得自己很愚蠢。他们猛烈抨击我们的身份和自我价值。所以我们尽量避开它们，就像回避我们在学校里从来不擅长的难题一样。这部分是我们大脑中工作时的情感部分。
那么，如何解释像Facebook这样的产品的兴起，这些产品让人类中的很大一部分人无意识地滚动浏览最容易被描述为垃圾内容的订阅源呢？嗯，我们人类并不完美。如果你有数十亿美元，一些最聪明的头脑和大量的数据可供你支配，你可以让很多人做你想做的事情。如果您将用户视为愚蠢和懒惰，那么在您的产品…的上下文中，您可能会将他们变成愚蠢和懒惰的人。但这是另一个帖子的主题。
首先，产品绝对应该尽可能简单。因为我尊重用户的时间，而不是因为我看不起他们的智慧。
第二，对人们的行为有一个理论。我是自决理论的铁杆粉丝，该理论认为人们重视自主权、关联性和能力。我喜欢帮助人们改善所有这三个维度的建筑产品。
第三，为你的产品制定一套原则。例如，在自决的三个轴心中，我特别关心自治(控制)。我发现好的产品，那些尊重用户的产品，给了他们更多的控制权。劣质产品会夺走控制权。简单性可以同时满足这两个目的。它可以通过将人们不关心的事情抽象出来，帮助他们集中注意力，从而让他们获得控制权。或者，它可以通过只让用户做产品设计者想让他们做的事情来夺走控制权。这就是我的原则之一：给人控制权。帮助他们做他们想做的事情，而不是你想让他们做的事情。
让我们尊重我们的用户。技术可以把我们最好的一面或最坏的一面带出来，无论是个人的还是集体的。让我们把重点放在最好的。
note_en:
It’s a common narrative in tech to design products with the assumption that users are stupid and lazy. I think that is both disrespectful and wrong.
 The idea is rooted in a lot of research around product usability, but it has been bastardized. Thing of it as a perversion of the  Don’t Make Me Think thesis.
 Don’t Make Me Think, the seminal web usability book by Steve Krug, tells us that products should be as simple as possible for users to use. Products shouldn’t be self-explanatory (ie understandable given a set of instructions), they should be self-evident (ie so obvious that they can be used  without having to read instructions). A good door has a push/pull sign to make it self-explanatory, but it still requires you to read and think. An even better door wouldn’t even need that label at all—you know what to do instinctively.
 But somehow, we’ve perverted that idea. Users are lazy, even stupid, we say. They just want to flick their fingers down an infinite feed, letting their eyes wander from item to item.
 But in  Don’t Make Me Think, Krug never refers to users in a derogatory way. He tells us how good products should work, and why basic psychology supports that. People want to reduce cognitive friction as much as possible. People don’t like unneeded cognitive friction. People skim quickly and “muddle through” products. And, most of all, people won’t undertake effort unless they believe it’s worth the cost. These are all facts backed by usability research and psychology.
 In other words, he tells us  what good products should look like, and  how people use them. But he doesn’t pass judgment on users. That’s up to us.
 And so naturally, we apply our view of the world, our values. If you view your users with contempt, then the reason behind why people don’t like complicated products is because they are stupid and lazy. If, on the other hand, you  respect your users, you might view things differently.
 Firstly, our brains have been wired, through millions of years of evolution, to conserve effort and time. That’s actually not being lazy, it’s being smart and protective of one of our most valuable assets. Naturally, we don’t undertake an activity unless we believe it’s worth the cost (though there are ways to trick us, more on that later). And if it takes effort to even figure out how much effort an activity will require, we’ll avoid that activity altogether. That’s the functional, practical piece of our brain at work.
 Secondly, we are a complex bundle of emotions. Even if we’re smart, we don’t like  feeling stupid. And complex, difficult things makes us feel stupid. They strike at our very identity and self-worth. So we try to avoid them like we avoid that hard topic we were never good at in school.  That part is the emotional piece of our brain at work.
 So what explains the rise of products like Facebook, which have gotten a large part of humanity mindlessly scrolling through feeds of what can most easily be described as garbage content? Well, we humans aren’t perfect. If you’ve got billions of dollars, some of the brightest minds, and a lot of data at your disposal, you can get a lot of people to do what you want. If you treat users as stupid and lazy, you can turn them into stupid and lazy people in the context of your product… but that’s a subject for another post.
  Firstly, products should definitely be as simple as possible. Because I  respect users’ time, not because I look down on their intelligence.
 Second, have a theory of how people behave. I’m a big fan of  Self-determination Theory, which states that people value autonomy, relatedness, and competence. And I love building product that help people improve all those three dimensions.
 Three, have a set of principles for your product. For instance, of the three axes of self-determination, I particularly care about autonomy (control). And I’ve found that good products, ones that respect their users,  give them more control. Bad products  take away control. Simplicity can fulfill both of those purposes. It can give people control by abstracting away things they don’t care about and helping them focus. Or it can take away control by only letting users do things the product’s designers want them to do. So that’s one of my principles: give people control. Help them do things  they want to do, not things  you want them to do.
 Let’s respect our users. Technology can bring out the best or worst in us, both individually and collectively. Let’s focus on the best.
title: 不尊重设计-用户并不愚蠢或懒惰
title_en: Disrespectful Design – Users aren’t stupid or lazy

note:
自2018年特朗普竞选团队成立了一家名为“美国制造媒体咨询公司”(American Made Media Consulters)的空壳公司以来，几乎不可能知道竞选团队向谁支付了费用、支付了什么以及支付了多少钱。自该公司被描述为“负责以公平市场价值安排和执行媒体购买和相关服务的供应商”以来，几乎不可能知道竞选团队向谁支付了费用。但是，5月27日，总部位于奥斯汀的广告经纪和软件公司Phunware的首席执行官艾伦·基尼托夫斯基(Alan Knitowski)宣布，“与美国制造媒体咨询公司(American Made Media Consulters)就特朗普-彭斯2020连任竞选活动的移动应用产品组合的开发、推出以及持续管理和发展建立了战略合作关系。”尽管Phunware从未出现在竞选活动的F.E.C.报告中，但Phunware的SEC文件显示，自去年以来，AMMC已经支付了大约400万美元。
从表面上看，Phunware似乎是开发该活动应用程序的一个奇怪的选择。在为特朗普总统工作之前，Phunware的软件在相对较少的应用程序中使用，最受欢迎的是星座应用程序。而且，自2019年以来，它一直卷入与优步(Uber)的诉讼，优步曾是该公司广告植入业务的客户。这场纠纷源于两名前Phunware员工长达一年的调查，他们发现，该公司假装在CNN等网站上投放优步广告，而实际上，这些广告即使真的出现在色情网站上，也是出现在色情网站上。但是，根据前Phunware员工和商业伙伴的说法，该公司对特朗普竞选团队的价值并不在于软件开发。“特朗普竞选团队不会为一款应用程序付给Phunware 400万美元，”该公司的一位前商业伙伴告诉我。“他们是在为数据付费。他们正在为定向广告服务付费。想象一下，如果每次我打开手机，我都会看到一条竞选信息，乔·拜登的美国意味着我们将在街头开战。这是特朗普竞选团队和布拉德·帕斯卡尔(Brad Parscale)-特朗普竞选团队负责数据和数字运营的高级顾问-从Phunware购买的服务。一款应用程序只是套餐的一部分。“。
特朗普2020应用程序本身就是一个大规模的数据收集工具。4月23日，当它上线时，时任特朗普竞选经理的Parscale敦促他在Facebook上的追随者“下载开创性的特朗普2020官方应用--不像你见过的其他蹩脚的政治应用。”尽管大肆炒作，2020年的应用程序概括了2016年应用程序的许多功能。有一个新闻源，上面有特朗普的社交媒体帖子、活动日历和录制的视频。2016年这款应用与众不同的“游戏”功能依然突出--通过分享联系人或筹款积累10万分的“特朗普军队”成员将被许诺与总统合影留念，而其他成员则可以使用积分获得装备折扣。“特朗普的军队”成员通过分享联系人或筹集资金积累了10万分，并被许诺与总统合影留念，而其他成员则可以使用积分获得装备折扣。用户会被提示邀请朋友下载这款应用-更多积分！-还可以使用这款应用注册，代表竞选团队打电话，成为民调观察员，登记选民，以及获得虚拟和面对面活动的门票。
2020年应用程序上最明显的新功能是新闻直播，这是由竞选团队精心策划的，以推动总统的谈话要点。它由一群竞选代理人主持，包括埃里克·特朗普(Eric Trump)的妻子劳拉·特朗普(Lara Trump)和小唐纳德·特朗普(Donald Trump，Jr.)的女友、竞选团队的国家财务主席金伯利·吉尔福伊尔(Kimberly Guilfoyle)。也有针对特定人口群体的频道，其中包括女性为特朗普提供的频道，黑人之声为特朗普提供的频道，以及拉丁裔为特朗普提供的频道。虽然这是传统新闻媒体的粗略近似，但特朗普的应用程序使用户能够完全与世隔绝地呆在事实可选的特朗普世界中。“我认为我们所做的一切都是为了对抗媒体，”Parscale在6月对路透表示.。“这是工具棚里的另一个打这场仗的工具，而且是一个很大的工具。”今年5月，在推特(Twitter)将特朗普的一条推文贴上违反其标准的标签后，引发了自由派媒体对保守派进行审查的新说法(尽管这条推文没有被删除)，这款竞选应用程序的下载量飙升。
要访问特朗普的应用程序，用户必须与竞选团队共享他们的手机号码。Parscale告诉路透社：“政治中最重要、最重要的事情就是手机号码。”“当我们收到手机号码时，它真的能让我们在数据库中识别它们。他们是谁，投票历史，一切。“。共和党私营数据公司Data Trust的首席运营官迈克尔·马里纳西奥(Michael Marinaccio)最近表示，“今年的新情况，或者至少是一种紧迫感，是在选民档案数据中尽可能多地获取手机号码。”要做到这一点，一个有效的方法是吸引支持者不仅与竞选团队分享他们自己的手机号码，而且还分享他们联系人的手机号码。移动分析公司Apptopia的首席执行官埃利兰·萨皮尔(Eliran Sapir)估计，140万应用程序下载量可能会
Phunware代表特朗普竞选团队收集的数据可能也超出了这款应用的范围。根据Phunware的首席运营官兰德尔·克劳德(Randall Crowder)的说法，该公司已经创建了一种“数据交换”，“使数字营销人员能够在几分钟内使用地理、兴趣、意图和人口细分……来设计定制受众。”高质量的全球定位系统位置数据来自美国的1亿多个设备，以增加基于位置的受众规模。“。在宣传材料中，该公司还声称拥有全球超过10亿台移动设备的独特设备标识，并开发了所谓的“知识图谱”--从手机和平板电脑上提取的数据，“以消费者为中心，收集行动、偏好、特征和预测的行为”。Facebook的社交图被描述为“每个人以及他们之间的关系的全球地图”，这就像Facebook的社交图一样，使该公司能够快速整理大型数据集，揭示原本会被遮蔽的联系和关系。例如：独居、很少投票、拥有枪支、生活在边境州的中年妇女。
那么，Phunware是如何获得10亿个独特的设备ID的呢？正如该公司向美国证券交易委员会描述的那样，这些信息是从使用Phunware软件的手机和平板电脑上收集的。但是，据与该公司共事的人说，除了通过其软件获得的数据外，Phunware还一直将其广告投放业务用作批发数据挖掘业务。例如，当它竞标在潘多拉(Pandora)等应用程序中投放广告时，它会抓起可能接触到广告的每一部手机和平板电脑的ID，即使它在竞标中落败。通过收集和存储这些信息，该公司能够汇编那些设备上下载的每个应用程序的相当全面的图片，以及用户为了使用该应用程序而共享的任何注册数据。
这些信息可以产生丰富的人口统计数据。例如，如果一项活动正在寻找对枪支有亲和力的年轻人，它可能会看看谁同时下载了Call of Duty和CCW，这是一款隐藏的Carry 50 State应用程序。然后，使用与设备ID相关联的位置数据，可以取消屏蔽数据并将其链接到个人。一旦竞选团队知道某人是谁，以及某人住在哪里，就不难开始建立选民档案，并使用这些信息来定制广告和信息。
联邦通信委员会前主席汤姆·惠勒告诉我，“这些都是类似剑桥分析的技术。它从多个来源收集数据的描述性力量，其中大多数消费者甚至不知道正在收集。这就是“剑桥分析”所做的。“。
7月下旬，以路易斯安那州共和党参议员比尔·卡西迪(Bill Cassidy)和俄勒冈州民主党同僚罗恩·怀登(Ron Wyden)为首的一群议员致函联邦贸易委员会(Federal Trade Commission)主席，要求他调查以这种方式使用投标信息是否构成不公平和欺骗性的做法。他们写道，“很少有美国人意识到，公司正在抽走并共享‘双向流’数据，以汇编关于它们的详尽档案，然后在没有法院命令的情况下，这些档案被出售给对冲基金、政治竞选活动，甚至卖给政府。”据数据市场Kochava的首席执行官查尔斯·曼宁(Charles Manning)说，“似乎没有任何监管机构意识到数字广告运营的技术基础。这是一项挑战，因为在不了解程序性广告是如何买卖的情况下，监管机构面临着一场艰苦的战斗，因为监管机构将面临一场艰苦的战斗，因为监管涉及的是不透明的供应链，在这些供应链上，欺诈行为可能会猖獗。
至少，特朗普的应用程序明确说明了它对用户的期望：“你可能会被要求提供某些信息，包括你的名字、用户名、密码、电子邮件、出生日期、性别、地址、就业信息和其他描述性信息，”该应用程序的隐私政策写道，“你可能会被要求提供某些信息，包括你的名字、用户名、密码、电子邮件、出生日期、性别、地址、就业信息和其他描述性信息。”“[应用程序的]服务可能包括依赖于使用存储在您的移动设备上或通过您的移动设备提供的信息的功能…”我们是…。保留存储有关您通过服务联系的人员的任何信息的权利。我们保留出于任何合法目的向DJTFP附属委员会和第三方使用、共享、交换和/或披露您的任何信息的权利。“。(当我问伍利竞选活动为什么要求支持者分享他们的联系人，因为它已经可以通过应用程序的权限访问他们时，他指出，当用户共享他们的联系人以赚取积分时，“这实际上是向你的联系人发送消息，要求他们下载这款应用程序。”因此，他们不仅可以获得你朋友和家人的数据，还可以利用你作为推荐人与他们取得联系。“)。

note_en:
Since the Trump campaign set up a shell company called  American Made Media Consultants, in 2018, an entity it describes as a “vendor responsible for arranging and executing media buys and related services at fair market value,” it’s been nearly impossible to know whom the campaign is paying, for what, and how much. But, on May 27th, Alan Knitowski, the C.E.O. of Phunware, an Austin-based ad broker and software company,  announced a “strategic relationship with American Made Media Consultants on the development, launch and ongoing management and evolution of the Trump-Pence 2020 Reelection Campaign’s mobile application portfolio.” Although Phunware never showed up in the campaign’s F.E.C.  reports, Phunware’s S.E.C. filings show that, since last year,  it has been paid around four million dollars by A.M.M.C.
  On its face, Phunware seems like a strange choice to develop the campaign’s app. Before working for President Trump, Phunware’s software was being used in relatively few applications, the most popular being a horoscope app. And, since 2019, it has been  embroiled in a  lawsuit with Uber, a former client of the company’s ad-placement business. The dispute stems from a yearlong investigation by two former Phunware employees who discovered that the company was pretending to place Uber ads on Web sites like CNN when, in fact, they were appearing on pornography sites, among others, if they appeared at all. But, according to former Phunware employees and business associates, the company’s value to the Trump campaign is not in software development. “The Trump campaign is not paying Phunware four million dollars for an app,” a former business partner of the company told me. “They are paying for data. They are paying for targeted advertising services. Imagine if every time I open my phone I see a campaign message that Joe Biden’s America means we’re going to have war in the streets. That’s the service the Trump campaign and Brad Parscale”—the Trump campaign’s senior adviser for data and digital operations—“have bought from Phunware. An app is just part of the package.”
 The Trump 2020 app is a massive data-collection tool in its own right. When it  launched, on April 23rd,  Parscale, who was  then Trump’s campaign manager, urged his followers on  Facebook to “download the groundbreaking Official Trump 2020 App—unlike other lame political apps you’ve seen.” Despite the hype, the 2020 app recapitulates many of the functions found on the  2016 app. There’s a news feed with Trump’s social-media posts, an events calendar, and recorded videos. The “gaming” features that distinguished the 2016 app are still prominent—a “Trump’s army” member who accumulates a hundred thousand points by sharing contacts or raising money is promised a photograph with the President, while other members can use points to get discounts on   gear. Users are prompted to invite friends to download the app—more points!—and can use the app to sign up to make calls on behalf of the campaign, to be a poll watcher, to register voters, and to get tickets to virtual and in-person events.
  The most obvious new feature on the 2020 app is a live news broadcast, carefully curated by the campaign to push the President’s talking points. It is hosted by a cast of campaign surrogates, including Lara Trump, Eric Trump’s wife, and  Kimberly Guilfoyle, Donald Trump, Jr.,’s girlfriend and the campaign’s national finance chair. There are also channels aimed at particular demographic groups, among them Women for Trump, Black Voices for Trump, and Latinos for Trump. Though it is a crude approximation of a traditional news outlet, the Trump app enables users to stay fully sequestered within the fact-optional Trump universe. “I think everything we do is to counter the media,”  Parscale told Reuters in June. “This is another tool in the tool shed to fight that fight, and it’s a big tool.” In May, after Twitter labelled one of Trump’s tweets as being  in violation of its standards, sparking renewed claims of liberal-media censorship of conservatives (despite the fact that the tweet was not taken down), downloads of the campaign app  soared.
 To access the Trump app, users must share their cell-phone numbers with the campaign. “The most important, golden thing in politics is a cellphone number,”  Parscale told Reuters. “When we receive cellphone numbers, it really allows us to identify them across the databases. Who are they, voting history, everything.”  Michael Marinaccio, the chief operating officer of Data Trust, a private Republican data company, said recently that “what’s new this year, or at least a sense of urgency, is getting as many cell-phone numbers as we can in the voter file data.” An effective way to do that is to entice supporters to share not only their own cell-phone numbers with the campaign but those of their contacts as well. One estimate, by  Eliran Sapir, the C.E.O. of Apptopia, a mobile-analytics company, is that 1.4 million app downloads could provide upward of a hundred million phone numbers. This will enable the Trump campaign to find and target people who have not consented to handing over their personal information. It’s not unlike how  Cambridge Analytica was able to harvest the data of nearly ninety million unsuspecting Facebook users, only this time it is one’s friends, family, and acquaintances who are willfully handing over the data for a chance to get a twenty-five-dollar discount on a   hat.
  By contrast, the new Biden app still collects data on users, but it outlines the specific uses of that data and doesn’t automatically collect the e-mail and phone numbers of users’ friends and family. “Unlike the Biden app, which seeks to provide users with awareness and control of the specific uses of their data, the Trump app collects as much as it can using an opt-out system and makes no promises as to the specific uses of that data,”  Samuel Woolley, the director of the propaganda research project at the University of Texas’s Center for Media Engagement, told me. “They just try to get people to turn over as much as possible.”
 A Trump spokesperson told me, “The Trump 2020 app was built by Phunware as a one-stop destination with a variety of tools to get voters engaged with President Trump’s reëlection campaign.” Among its main contributions to the app’s data-mining capabilities is a “ location experience kit,” which the company had previously marketed to hospitals and malls to help people navigate unfamiliar buildings. Visitors could pair their phone’s Bluetooth with beacons set up throughout the facility. Initially, the Trump 2020 app was built around  big rallies, where this feature would have been useful. According to one former employee, however, the company’s location software, which  functions even when the app is not open, may be capable of sucking up more than geographic coördinates. It could potentially “sniff out all of the information you have on your phone. Any sort of registration data, your name, your phone number, potentially your Social Security number, and other pieces of data. It could sniff out how many apps you have on your phone, what type of apps you have on your phone, what apps you deleted recently, how much time you’ve spent in an app, and your dwell time at various specific locations. It could give a very intimate picture of that individual and their relationship with that mobile device.” (Phunware did not respond to multiple requests for comment.)
  In 2017, as Phunware was moving into the  election space, the company’s Web site announced, “As soon as the first few campaigns recognize the value of mobile ad targeting for voter engagement, the floodgates will open. Which campaign will get there first and strike it rich?” A year later, according to people familiar with the effort, the company used its location-tracking capabilities to create a lobbying campaign on behalf of a health-care company aiming to influence legislators in Georgia. It put a “geofence” around the governor’s mansion that recorded the I.D. of every device that went in and out of the building, and then used the I.D.s to send targeted messages to those phones (likely including the governor’s) about the legislation it was aiming to influence. The legislation passed. Phunware’s leadership has also discussed their ability to geofence polling places, according to people who were present during these discussions, in order to send targeted campaign ads to voters as they step into the voting booth. While it is illegal to advertise in the vicinity of the polls, using location data in this way to send targeted ads could enable a campaign to breach that border surreptitiously.
  Phunware’s data collection on behalf of the Trump campaign likely extends beyond the app as well. According to Phunware’s chief operating officer, Randall Crowder, the company has created a “data exchange” that “enables digital marketers to design custom audiences within minutes using geographic, interest, intent, and demographic segments . . . high-quality G.P.S. location data points from one hundred million-plus devices in the United States to increase scale of location-based audiences.” In its  promotional materials, the company also claims to have unique device I.D.s for more than a billion mobile devices worldwide, and to have developed what it calls a Knowledge Graph—a “consumer-centric collection of actions, preferences, characteristics and predicted behavior” from the data it has siphoned from mobile phones and tablets. Much like Facebook’s social graph, which has been  described as “the global mapping of everybody and how they’re related,” this enables the company to quickly sort through large data sets, uncovering connections and relations that otherwise would be obscured. For example: middle-aged women who live alone, rarely vote, own guns, and live in a border state.
 So how did Phunware obtain a billion unique device I.D.s? As the company described it to the S.E.C., they were collected from phones and tablets that use Phunware’s software. But, according to people who have worked with the company, in addition to the data it obtains through its software, Phunware has been using its ad-placement business as a wholesale data-mining operation. When it bids to place an ad in an app like, for example, Pandora, it scoops up the I.D. of every phone and tablet that would have been exposed to the ad, even if it loses the bid. By collecting and storing this information, the company is able to compile a fairly comprehensive picture of every app downloaded on those devices, and any registration data a user has shared in order to use the app.
 This information can yield rich demographic data. If a campaign is looking for young men with an affinity for guns, for instance, it might look at who has downloaded both Call of Duty and CCW, the Concealed Carry Fifty State app. Then, using the location data associated with the device I.D., the data can be unmasked and linked to an individual. Once a campaign knows who someone is, and where a person lives, it is not difficult to start building a voter file, and using this information to tailor ads and messages.
 Tom Wheeler, the former chair of the Federal Communications Commission, told me, “These are Cambridge Analytica-like techniques. It’s collecting the descriptive power of data from multiple sources, most of which the consumer doesn’t even know are being collected. And that’s what Cambridge Analytica did.”
 In late July, a group of lawmakers, led by Senator Bill Cassidy, Republican of Louisiana, and his Democratic colleague Ron Wyden, of Oregon, sent  a letter to the chair of the Federal Trade Commission asking him to investigate whether using bidding information in this way constitutes an unfair and deceptive practice. “Few Americans realize that companies are siphoning off and sharing that ‘bidstream’ data to compile exhaustive dossiers about them,” they wrote, “which are then sold to hedge funds, political campaigns, and even to the government without court orders.” According to Charles Manning, the C.E.O. of Kochava, a data marketplace, “There are no regulatory bodies that appear to be aware of the technological foundations upon which digital advertising operates. This is a challenge, because without understanding how programmatic ads are bought and sold, regulators face an uphill battle in applying regulation that deals with opaque supply chains where fraudulent behavior can flourish.”
  The Trump app, at least, is explicit about what it expects from its users: “You may be asked to provide certain information, including your name, username, password, e-mail, date of birth, gender, address, employment information, and other descriptive information,” the app’s privacy policy states. “The Services [of the app] may include features that rely on the use of information stored on, or made available through, your mobile Device. . . . We . . . reserve the right to store any information about the people you contact via the Services. . . . We reserve the right to use, share, exchange and/or disclose to DJTFP affiliated committee and third parties any of your information for any lawful purpose.” (When I asked Woolley why the campaign was asking supporters to share their contacts, since it already had access to them through the app’s permissions, he pointed out that, when a user shares their contacts to earn points, “that actually sends out messages to your contacts asking them to download the app. So rather than just getting data on your friends and family, they are able to also reach out to them using you as a reference.”)
 The policy also notes that the campaign will be collecting information gleaned from G.P.S. and other location services, and that users will be tracked as they move around the Internet. Users also agree to give the campaign access to the phone’s Bluetooth connection, calendar, storage, and microphone, as well as permission to read the contents of their memory card, modify or delete the contents of the card, view the phone status and identity, view its Wi-Fi connections, and prevent the phone from going to sleep. These permissions give the Trump data operation access to the intimate details of users’ lives, the ability to listen in on those lives, and to follow users everywhere they go. It’s a colossal—and essentially free—data-mining enterprise. As Woolley and his colleague Jacob Gursky  wrote in  MIT Technology Review, the Trump 2020 app is “a voter surveillance tool of extraordinary power.”
 I learned this firsthand after downloading the Trump 2020 app on a burner phone I bought in order to examine it, using an alias and a new e-mail address. Two days later, the President sent me a note, thanking me for joining his team. Lara Trump invited me (for a small donation) to become a Presidential adviser. Eric Trump called me one of his father’s “FIERCEST supporters from the beginning.” But the messages I began getting from the Trump campaign every couple of hours were sent not only to the name and address I’d used to access the app. They were also sent to the e-mail address and name associated with the credit card I’d used to buy the phone and its   card, neither of which I had shared with the campaign. Despite my best efforts, they knew who I was and where to reach me.
   To understand the path Donald Trump has taken to the 2020 election,  look at what he has provided the executive class.

title: 特朗普竞选团队的移动应用程序收集大量数据
title_en: Trump campaign's mobile app collecting huge amount of data
